_meta.description: A few well-known Tony Hansen / Digital Fire cone-6 base recipes by code, as starting points for formulation. Amounts are batch percentages (may total slightly over 100). Verify live at digitalfire.com/recipe/<code> before relying on them.
_meta.usage: Feed 'materials' into umf.py / cte.py, or scale with convert.py batch. Add colorants/opacifiers via 'additions'.

G2926B.name: G2926B - Cone 6 transparent glossy (whiteware/porcelain)
G2926B.description: Fires high-gloss and ultra-clear with low melt mobility. A reliable, balanced cone-6 transparent base; the standard starting point for cone-6 liner and colored glossy glazes.

G2934.name: G2934 - Cone 6 matte (MgO)
G2934.description: A cone-6 magnesia matte base (silky/dolomite matte family). Matteness comes from MgO, not underfiring. Good for non-functional surfaces; test for cutlery marking/food safety before functional use.
G2934.note: Amounts are a representative version; confirm exact recipe at the source URL.

Celadon.name: Cone 6 Celadon (iron)
Celadon.description: A clear, slightly fluid glossy base with 1.5% iron. In OXIDATION (electric) reads pale honey/green-celadon; in SAGGAR/local REDUCTION the iron converts toward FeO and gives the classic cool blue-green celadon. Tuned for fit (CTE ~7.3) and clarity (SiO2:Al2O3 ~10:1). Apply medium-thin over white porcelain; pools darker, breaks lighter on edges.
Celadon.note: Original composition for this repo (not a published Hansen code). For a stronger green-blue use 1-2% RIO; for a darker 'northern' celadon push 2-3%. Verify fit on your body and test for crazing.
Celadon.tuning: umf.py -> SiO2:Al2O3 ~10:1, fluxes CaO-dominant with some MgO; cte.py ~7.3 x10^-6.

Satin.name: Cone 6 Satin Matte (calcium-magnesium)
Satin.description: A soft, silky SATIN (between glossy and full matte) from a calcium-magnesium flux balance and a low SiO2:Al2O3 ratio - not from underfiring. Pleasant on functional ware but test for cutlery marking and food safety before functional use. Takes colorants in muted, soft tones.
Satin.note: Original composition for this repo. Matteness comes from high CaO+MgO and SiO2:Al2O3 ~7:1 with low absolute silica; a slow cool deepens the satin. For more matte, raise dolomite/EPK; for more gloss, raise silica.
Satin.tuning: umf.py -> SiO2:Al2O3 ~7.5:1, MgO ~0.19, R2O:RO ~0.13:0.87; cte.py ~7.3 x10^-6, good fit.

CopperRedSaggarSlip.name: Copper-Red / Flashing Saggar Slip
CopperRedSaggarSlip.description: A SLIP (engobe), not a melting glaze - brushed/sprayed thin onto bisque (or burnished leather-hard) porcelain, then SAGGAR-fired with combustibles so local reduction flashes the copper to reds/pinks/iridescent metallics. Results are unpredictable and one-of-a-kind by design, and reoxidize easily. DECORATIVE ONLY - not food safe (copper + porous unmelted slip).
CopperRedSaggarSlip.note: Tin helps the copper develop red. UMF/CTE are NOT the right lens for a slip - do not read umf.py/cte.py output as a glaze. Vary copper 2-6% and pack the saggar with sawdust/salt/copper for flashing. See firing-and-atmosphere/saggar-firing.md for loading + SAFETY (CO + copper fumes -> ventilate).
CopperRedSaggarSlip.source: Composition for this repo; saggar/flashing technique per firing-and-atmosphere skill.
